title: iliamna remota var. typica
intro: Iliamna remota var. typica, also known as Mountain Mahogany or Mountain Globe Mallow, is a perennial plant species native to North America. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Iliamna remota var. typica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used topically in ointments and salves to treat skin conditions and promote tissue repair.
